---
description: "Structure and content requirements for technical constitution documents generated by the CodeWith Accelerate workflow"
applyTo: '**/constitution*.md'
---

# Constitution File Conventions

Apply these conventions when generating or editing constitution.md files.

## Structure Requirements

- Use exactly 18 numbered sections.
- Include a complete Table of Contents with section links at the top of the document.
- Use consistent markdown formatting throughout.
- Number sections sequentially from 1 to 18.

## Content Requirements

- Include exact version numbers for all dependencies in the Technology Stack section.
- Document all interfaces with complete member signatures in Section 14.
- Provide runnable code examples (minimum 3 examples, 40+ lines each) in Section 12.
- Sections 13-17 are migration-critical and must contain complete contracts.
- Document all enum values with numeric assignments in Section 15.
- Include license information for all third-party libraries.

## Section Completeness Standards

| Section | Minimum Content |
|---------|----------------|
| 12. Code Examples | 3 runnable examples, 40+ lines each |
| 14. Interface Contracts | Every interface with all members |
| 15. Enum Definitions | Every enum with all values and numeric assignments |
| 16. Model Definitions | Every model with all properties and types |
| 17. Base Classes | Every base class with extension points and virtual members |

## Privacy and Security

- Never include API keys, secrets, or credentials.
- Never expose proprietary algorithms or patented processes.
- Warn about licensed dependencies that require purchase.
- Include a proprietary screening checklist at the end of the document.

## Quality Standards

- All code examples must be complete and runnable.
- All interfaces must include all members with type signatures.
- All models must include all properties with types.
- Testing strategies must include framework names, patterns, and base test classes.
